capabilities.label: Capability truth matrix
capabilities.notes: Build fails when declared capabilities lack verifiable state, source files, or required tests.

capabilities.label: WP PFWorkflow dependency detection
capabilities.notes: Detects active workflow plugin from PFW_REST_NAMESPACE/class, reads namespace from WordPress when available, and falls back only when absent.

capabilities.label: Typed Workflow API client
capabilities.notes: Mock tests execute the compiled TypeScript client and verify endpoints, request bodies, nonce, normalized errors, retry, abort and timeline.

capabilities.label: List workflows from Workflow plugin
capabilities.notes: Runtime smoke verifies GET /wp-pfworkflow/v1/workflows returns HTTP 200 inside WordPress Docker.

capabilities.label: Get workflow by id
capabilities.notes: Mock test verifies endpoint shape; Docker smoke verifies GET on a temporary workflow created from a real template.

capabilities.label: List templates from Workflow plugin
capabilities.notes: Runtime smoke verifies GET /wp-pfworkflow/v1/templates returns HTTP 200 and exposes templates array. The agent does not duplicate workflow template catalog data.

capabilities.label: Create draft workflow from selected template
capabilities.notes: Uses graph returned by Workflow API template catalog; Docker smoke creates a temporary draft workflow and deletes it afterward. Playwright verifies template preview does not POST and Create draft loads the created workflow id.

capabilities.label: Validate workflow graph
capabilities.notes: Docker smoke validates the graph from a real template after creating a temporary draft workflow.

capabilities.label: Dry-run workflow
capabilities.notes: Docker smoke calls test-run on a temporary workflow. This is dry-run, not real run.

capabilities.label: Run workflow
capabilities.notes: Mock test verifies endpoint, request and response handling. Docker smoke intentionally does not execute real run to avoid workflow side effects.

capabilities.label: Read workflow logs
capabilities.notes: Mock test verifies query params; Docker smoke verifies logs endpoint returns an array.

capabilities.label: Read Workflow node catalog
capabilities.notes: Agent tool calls the real Workflow REST catalog route. The agent does not bundle or duplicate the node catalog.

capabilities.label: Read Workflow studio context
capabilities.notes: Agent can inspect Workflow studio context, token language, site data and current Workflow capabilities through Workflow REST.

capabilities.label: List Workflow studio async option providers
capabilities.notes: Agent can list async option providers exposed by Workflow instead of hardcoding option sources.

capabilities.label: Resolve Workflow studio async options
capabilities.notes: Agent can resolve dynamic option values through Workflow REST using provider keys returned by /studio/options.

capabilities.label: Inspect Workflow node contract
capabilities.notes: Agent can inspect a node contract and execution metadata before proposing or running node-level operations.

capabilities.label: Dry-run Workflow node
capabilities.notes: Agent can dry-run a node through Workflow REST. This tool is read-only and must not be treated as execution.

capabilities.label: Execute Workflow node
capabilities.notes: Agent can request isolated node execution through Workflow REST, but the tool is side-effectful and runtime confirmation is required.

capabilities.label: Export workflow
capabilities.notes: Agent can export workflows through the real Workflow REST export route.

capabilities.label: Update workflow
capabilities.notes: Agent can update workflows through Workflow REST, but the tool is side-effectful and runtime confirmation is required.

capabilities.label: Import workflow
capabilities.notes: Agent can import workflows through Workflow REST, but the tool is side-effectful and runtime confirmation is required.

capabilities.label: Read workflow execution support diagnostics
capabilities.notes: Agent can fetch Workflow execution support diagnostics by workflow id and execution id.

capabilities.label: Main chat surface consumes the agent runtime
capabilities.notes: The visible chat input posts to /agent-runtime/turn with {providerId, model, message}. When no provider is active the column shows the ProviderWizard instead of the composer. Side-effect tools surface inline Confirmar/Cancelar buttons backed by the runtime confirmation gate. The legacy command parser was removed.

capabilities.label: Agent chat shell
evidence: live cert iter 2 (deepseek-v4-flash): round-trip + multi-round tool calls + side-effect confirmation gate verified end-to-end
capabilities.notes: Functional agent chat shell: drives the Framework Loop via /agent-runtime/turn-v2 (round-trip, multi-round tool calls, side-effect confirmation gate). Promoted from scaffolded after end-to-end certification (Slice 7 iter 2).

capabilities.label: Workflow read-only Rete canvas
capabilities.notes: Playwright verifies render with nodes/connections, read-only inspector, blocked connection creation, local-only node movement, workflow switch cleanup and desktop screenshot.

capabilities.label: Preview Workflow API templates before creation
capabilities.notes: Template list, search/filter and preview use data received from Workflow API. The test rejects hardcoded behavior by serving mock templates and asserting preview causes no POST while Create draft calls POST /workflows.

capabilities.label: API provider preset registry
capabilities.notes: Registry is loaded through Agent REST API. Tests reject defaultModels and non-empty modelHints so models cannot be hardcoded as source of truth.

capabilities.label: Typed Agent provider API client
capabilities.notes: Mock tests verify exact endpoints, nonce, request bodies, ProjectFlash contract fetch, OpenAPI contract fetch, agent tool catalog fetch, model discovery, manual model entry, provider health, LLM gateway calls, agent runtime turn and normalized permission errors.

capabilities.label: Provider settings UI
capabilities.notes: Playwright verifies provider UI loads presets from REST, starts not_configured, saves without rendering full key, validates, deletes, avoids localStorage secrets and makes no LLM generation calls. Model display uses Agent API responses rather than bundled lists.

capabilities.label: Encrypted provider credential store
capabilities.notes: Docker smoke verifies manage_options protection, AES-256-GCM metadata, no full API key in REST responses or stored option JSON, delete and redacted status payloads.

capabilities.label: Provider connection test through model discovery
capabilities.notes: Connection test now delegates to provider health, which uses backend model discovery endpoint only. It does not call chat/completions, responses, messages, generateContent or other generation endpoints.

capabilities.label: Provider model discovery
capabilities.notes: Docker smoke verifies OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic-compatible and Gemini-compatible model discovery from API-shaped responses. It rejects provider auth failures and returns no fallback list.

capabilities.label: Provider model discovery cache
capabilities.notes: Cache uses a 600-second TTL and marks returned catalog/model entries as cache. The smoke asserts the cached read does not perform a provider HTTP request.

capabilities.label: Manual provider model entries
capabilities.notes: Manual model entry is accepted only for explicit manual-capable discovery modes such as api_or_manual and rejected for API-only presets.

capabilities.label: Provider health check
capabilities.notes: Docker smoke verifies connected, auth, quota, rate limit, invalid model and network classifications, plus validation state updates without leaking secrets.

capabilities.label: Provider LLM generation runtime smoke
capabilities.notes: Mock Docker smoke verifies model discovery plus one generation call for OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic-compatible and Gemini-compatible families, parses outputs and checks REST payloads do not leak secrets. Real-provider smoke is optional because it depends on local stored credentials and external provider balances.

capabilities.label: Internal LLM gateway
capabilities.notes: The live LLM gateway is the Framework one (Framework\Llm\Gateway), driven by the Loop. Certified end-to-end in runtime-compaction-real-deepseek, where the Loop calls gateway->complete() against the real DeepSeek API and completes multi-round turns. The old SPA-side complete/toolCall/stream surface against /llm/* was removed in the Framework migration (those endpoints no longer exist).

capabilities.label: Machine-readable Agent REST contract
capabilities.notes: Exports schema, routes, permissions, capability matrix, provider preset metadata, agent tools and Workflow dependency status without credential values. Docker smoke verifies permission guard, route registration parity and secret redaction.

capabilities.label: OpenAPI Agent REST contract
capabilities.notes: Exports OpenAPI 3.1 paths, security scheme, operation metadata and component schemas from the same route inventory used by the ProjectFlash contract. Docker smoke verifies route parity and no credential leaks.

capabilities.label: Generated internal agent documentation
capabilities.notes: Generates documentation from the live Agent contract on each request, including route/tool/provider summaries without stored credential values.

capabilities.label: Deterministic agent fix suggestions
capabilities.notes: Returns rule-based suggestions from supplied errors, evidence and timeline. It does not invent fixes through LLM text.

capabilities.label: Minimal agent runtime
capabilities.notes: Docker smoke verifies LLM-selected declared tools, rejection of undeclared tools, Workflow API execution, timeline events, confirmation before side effects, and no Workflow API call without provider credential.

capabilities.label: Per-bucket REST rate limiter
capabilities.notes: Per-user fixed-window limiter backed by WordPress transients. Buckets: reads (60/min), config (20/min), llm (10/min), agent_turn (10/min). Returns HTTP 429 with retryAfter when exhausted. Defaults are filterable via pfa_rate_limit_defaults.

capabilities.label: Negative security matrix smoke
capabilities.notes: Single smoke that proves: every admin route rejects anonymous callers, cookie-auth without X-WP-Nonce returns 401/403, cookie-auth with valid nonce returns 200, rate limiter returns HTTP 429 after the bucket is exhausted, oversized POST bodies return HTTP 413, stored credential keys never appear in REST responses or in the option_value column, side-effect tools remain flagged for the runtime confirmation gate.

capabilities.label: Body-size guard for LLM and agent-runtime POST routes
capabilities.notes: POST handlers that accept prompts, tool definitions or fix-suggestion payloads reject any body larger than 524288 bytes with HTTP 413 pfa_payload_too_large. Defends against frontend bugs and accidental large pastes; the per-route rate limiter still applies.

capabilities.label: Beta readiness criteria endpoint
capabilities.notes: GET /agent-runtime/beta-readiness returns pass/fail per severe criterion of Sprint 20: no_hardcoded_primary_screen, no_done_without_real_api, user_can_inspect_agent_actions, partials_are_honest, declared_routes_are_registered. Plus per-state totals, partial capabilities list, providers summary (no keys) and workflow dependency state. The smoke asserts ready=true and that only ui.chat_shell is allowed at scaffolded; everything else must be tested.

capabilities.label: Diagnostic UI tab
capabilities.notes: Diagnostic tab consumes /agent-runtime/beta-readiness and renders status badge (beta ready vs partial), each severe criterion with PASS/FAIL flag, capability matrix totals, list of partial capabilities (only ui.chat_shell today), workflow dependency state and configured providers (no keys). Walkthrough Playwright captures the rendered tab into sprint-20-beta-walkthrough.png.

capabilities.label: End-to-end beta walkthrough
capabilities.notes: Playwright smoke walks the full beta scenario end-to-end with mocked REST: open drawer, select Agent tab, send message, observe ActionInspector status=completed plus executions and timeline, switch to Diagnostic tab, observe ready badge plus 5/5 PASS criteria. Asserts that exactly one /agent-runtime/turn POST landed and that the chat session was persisted via /chat-sessions/{id}/messages. Final screenshot captures the diagnostic state.

capabilities.label: WordPress release ZIP packaging
capabilities.notes: scripts/build-release.mjs reads .distignore (fnmatch-style globs), stages every included file under .release/staging/wp-pfagent/, then writes .release/wp-pfagent-<version>.zip. On Windows the ZIP is built through the .NET ZipArchive API directly so entry names use POSIX forward slashes (PHP ZipArchive on Linux requires this to recreate folders). Two smokes verify: the Node smoke extracts the ZIP and asserts required files, forbidden files, dist bundles, plugin header and absence of API-key shapes; the Docker smoke does the same plus php -l on every PHP file and walks the require_once tree from wp-pfagent.php to confirm every target exists in the ZIP.

capabilities.label: UX polish: states, errors, aria, keyboard, responsive medium
capabilities.notes: AgentConsole reuses a banner component for errors with role=alert, exposes Ctrl+Enter to send and Escape to clear, declares aria-label on every primary control, and shows a real loading spinner while the turn is in flight. App.tsx listens for Escape to close the open drawer. styles.css defines focus-visible outlines on tabs and buttons plus a @720px breakpoint that stacks the shell, hides redundant inspector columns and forces buttons to full width. Playwright smoke captures sprint-16-wide.png (1440x900) and sprint-16-medium.png (768x900), and asserts no truncated buttons, no console-extends-beyond-drawer overflow, aria labels on send/textarea, and Esc closes the drawer.

capabilities.label: Persistent agent trace log with classified kinds
capabilities.notes: Custom table wp_pfa_trace_log keyed on trace_id (UUIDv4), schema v2. AgentRuntime emits started/success/failed/needs_confirmation/rejected rows for kind=agent_turn, success/failed rows for kind=workflow_api, and success/failed rows for kind=llm_call (one per LlmGateway tool_call + one per final complete) carrying tokens_total + cost_micros per call. RestApi emits rate_limited rows for kind=rate_limit and failed rows for kind=body_size. Every persisted message + context_json is run through ChatSessions::redact_secrets at write time. Schema v2 added tokens_total INT and cost_micros BIGINT columns; pfa_trace_log_schema_version option triggers reinstall on upgrade.

capabilities.label: Diagnostic support export
capabilities.notes: GET /agent-runtime/support-export returns agent contract summary, workflow dependency state, environment (wpVersion, phpVersion, locale, timezone, multisite, debug), provider statuses with maskedKey only, recent traces (last 50 of current user), recent failures (last 20), and 24h aggregate metrics. The smoke asserts no raw API key string and no sk-... shape leaks anywhere in the export payload.

capabilities.label: Per-user trace aggregation metrics
capabilities.notes: GET /agent-runtime/metrics?windowHours=N (1..168) returns totals.byKind, byStatus, byProvider, totalRows, totalTokens, totalCostMicros, tokensByProvider and costMicrosByProvider for the current user inside the window. Defaults to 24 hours. costMicros is integer (1/1,000,000 of currency unit); today the gateway reports cost.amount=null so values stay 0 until pricing lands.

capabilities.label: Persistent chat sessions with cross-user isolation and secret redaction
capabilities.notes: REST + storage layer for chat sessions. CPT pfa_chat_session, post_status=private, owned by post_author. CRUD + append-messages REST endpoints. Default access policy denies cross-user reads/writes; pfa_chat_session_can_access filter allows hosts to grant cross-author access. Messages are redacted at save time for sk-..., sk-ant-api03-..., AIza..., glpat-..., ghp_..., xox*-... and Bearer ... patterns. Body-size guard at 512 KiB on create/patch/append. Delete is force-true. UI integration in App.tsx is parked: the previous AgentConsole wired auto-append per turn but was removed when the main chat moved to /agent-runtime/turn; the REST surface remains the persistent layer external consumers (and a future App.tsx re-wire) will use.

capabilities.label: Per-execution timestamps and workflow diff capture
capabilities.notes: Each execution returned by /agent-runtime/turn carries startedAt, endedAt, durationMs and status. Read-only tools (workflow_get_contract, workflow_list, workflow_get) have diff=null. Modifying tool workflow_apply includes diff with real before/after snapshots: payload.workflow.id<=0 yields before=null + changeType=created, positive id yields a real before snapshot via agent_workflow_full + changeType=updated. Snapshots come from the wp-pfworkflow PHP-internal service, not REST. Tool failures do not report status=completed.

capabilities.label: Workflow tool contracts (agent-ready surface)
capabilities.notes: Agent surface is 4 tools (workflow_get_contract, workflow_list, workflow_get, workflow_apply) backed by the wp-pfworkflow PHP-internal service exposed via apply_filters('projectflash_workflow_agent_api', null). Tool catalog validation accepts either documented REST endpoints or a phpService block. Docker smoke verifies success, envelope shape ({content, contextForYou}), service-failure propagation, and id<=0/positive create-or-update semantics through workflow_apply.

capabilities.label: wp-pfworkflow agent contract bootstrap
capabilities.notes: PHP-internal call to agent_contract() on the workflow service. Returns the type catalog, compatibility matrix, typed contract for every node, and global rules wrapped as { content, contextForYou }. Bootstrap-only: agent prompts the LLM to fetch once per conversation and reuse it from context.

capabilities.label: wp-pfworkflow agent workflow list
capabilities.notes: PHP-internal call to agent_workflow_list($filters). Returns ALL workflows as full round-trippable payloads (not ids). Outer envelope and each item are { content, contextForYou } — recursive. Filters: status, query.

capabilities.label: wp-pfworkflow agent single workflow
capabilities.notes: PHP-internal call to agent_workflow_full($id). Returns one full round-trippable workflow envelope including honestStatus per node. Used by snapshot_workflow() for diff capture in the action inspector.

capabilities.label: wp-pfworkflow agent unified create-or-update
capabilities.notes: PHP-internal call to agent_workflow_apply($payload). The id inside payload.workflow.id determines behavior: id<=0 (typically -1) creates, positive id updates. Side-effecting; the AgentRuntime confirmation gate fires before invocation. Construction only; no execution semantics.

capabilities.label: wp-pfmanagement typed agent contract
capabilities.notes: PHP-internal call to agent_contract() via projectflash_management_agent_api filter. Returns the field-type catalog + EQL operator vocabulary + supported resource kinds, wrapped as { content, contextForYou }.

capabilities.label: wp-pfmanagement kind-scoped list
capabilities.notes: PHP-internal call to agent_list(kind, filters) via projectflash_management_agent_api filter. kind chooses the resource family (entity, record, action, application, module, group, role, page).

capabilities.label: wp-pfmanagement kind+ref get
capabilities.notes: PHP-internal call to agent_get(kind, ref) via projectflash_management_agent_api filter. Returns the round-trippable payload for one resource (same shape pfm_apply accepts back).

capabilities.label: wp-pfmanagement unified create-or-update
capabilities.notes: PHP-internal call to agent_apply(kind, payload). Side-effecting; the AgentRuntime confirmation gate fires before invocation. Construction only; no execution semantics.

capabilities.label: Internal workflow authoring DSL (.pfflow)
capabilities.notes: JavaScript-subset DSL used internally between the authoring LLM and pfagent. The customer never sees the source. Auto-generates /lib/nodes.d.ts from wp-pfworkflow's contract. Roundtrip smoke verifies compile + decompile + recompile yields structurally equivalent graphs.

capabilities.label: Virtual filesystem for workflow source files (umbrella)
capabilities.notes: LLM file tools (list_files / read_file / write_file / edit_file / move_file / delete_file) operate on a virtual workspace whose contents the customer never sees. Write/edit update an in-progress draft that compiles to a live preview graph; the customer commits via the Apply button which calls POST /vfs/apply. Drafts persisted in option storage so reopening the chat restores them.

capabilities.label: VFS list_files tool
capabilities.notes: Enumerates the LLM virtual workspace; /lib/nodes.d.ts, /workflows/*, /templates/* with metadata.

capabilities.label: VFS read_file tool
capabilities.notes: Returns the current source for a workflow file (draft if one exists; otherwise the postmeta-cached live source).

capabilities.label: VFS write_file tool
capabilities.notes: Updates a draft + compiled preview without touching the live workflow. Returns typed compile errors on bad source.

capabilities.label: VFS edit_file tool (str-replace)
capabilities.notes: Surgical string replacement requiring the snippet to be unique in the file; compiles the result and updates draft+preview.

capabilities.label: VFS move_file tool (rename)
capabilities.notes: Renames a workflow's live name on the underlying record; side-effect, gated by the runtime confirmation modal.

capabilities.label: VFS delete_file tool
capabilities.notes: Deletes the underlying workflow; side-effect, gated by the runtime confirmation modal.
